definition.name: affinely_constrained_state_space
definition.definition: Given a unital *-algebra A with state space S(A), and self-adjoint elements a_1, ..., a_m ∈ A^{sa} with real scalars β_1, ..., β_m ∈ R, the affinely constrained state space is C = {φ ∈ S(A) : φ(a_j) = β_j, j = 1, ..., m}. More generally, one may consider inequality constraints C = {φ ∈ S(A) : φ(a_j) ≤ β_j} or mixed equality/inequality constraints. Since each map φ ↦ φ(a_j) is a continuous affine functional on S(A) (in the weak-* topology), C is a closed convex subset of S(A), hence compact. The integer m counts only the additional constraints beyond normalisation (φ(1) = 1 is already encoded in the definition of S(A)). This is the feasible set of an abstract noncommutative semidefinite program. Reference: Weis-Shirokov, J. Convex Anal. 28(3), 2021, §3.
